Cic..Back: Spasmodic symptoms of the back. Opisthotonos.
Cic..Back, pain: the head is drawn back opisthotonos all the limbs are convulsed and rigid.
Cic..Chest: Cold sensation in the chest. Spasms of the chest.
Cic..Extremities: All conditions of the limbs are of a spasmodic character.
Cic..Generalities: This remedy is of interest because of its convulsive tendency. It puts the whole nervous system in such a state of increased irritability that pressure on a part causes convulsions.
Cic..Head: Headache as if the brain were loose on walking. When thinking of the exact nature of the pain it ceased.
Cic..Mind: Wants to be alone; dislike to society. Singing, shouting, dancing; likes toys, jumps about like a child. Lies in bed lamenting and wailing. Great agitation; child grasps at one's clothing in a frightened manner.
Cic..Skin: It has troubles from shaving; it is useful in such eruptions as come in the whiskers; barber's itch; a solid crop of eruptions all over the face wherever the whiskers grow. Eruptions on the cheek like eczema.
Cic..Stomach: An aura in the stomach gives warning of the convulsion.
Cic..Stomach, distension: if the stomach is disordered or chilled, or if he has fear or other mental conditions, convulsions come on.
Cic..Stomach, pain: An aura in the stomach gives warning of the convulsion.
Cic..Abdomen, pain: With severe pains in the bowels come convulsive movements and convulsions.
Cic..Generalities, pain: there may be compression and yet all the pains be in distant parts; drawing of the muscles and cramps.
Cic..Appetite: This patient has strange desires; desires to eat coal and many other strange articles, because he is unable to distinguish between things edible and things unfit to be eaten; eats coal and raw potatoes.
Cic..Appetite, desires: This patient has strange desires; desires to eat coal and many other strange articles, because he is unable to distinguish between things edible and things unfit to be eaten; eats coal and raw potatoes.
Cic..Clinical: It has cured traumatic tetanus lockjaw, epilepsy, epileptiform convulsions.
Cic..Heart & Circulation, heart: Feels as if the heart stopped beating.
Cic..Abdomen: With severe pains in the bowels come convulsive movements and convulsions.
Cic..Back, dorsal region, back: Spasmodic symptoms of the back. Opisthotonos.
Cic..Heart & Circulation: Some complaints spread from the chest, especially from the heart; the rigors and chills begin in the chest; and there is a sensation of coldness about the heart; and from there it extends to other parts.
Cic..Back, dorsal region: Spasmodic symptoms of the back. Opisthotonos.
Cic..Face: Eruptions on the cheek like eczema. Swelling of the submaxillary glands. Erysipelatous eruptions.
Cic..Generalities, lying: He recollects nothing that took place or that he said during a certain period. He knows nobody, and lies without recognizing anyone, but when asked questions he answers correctly.
Cic..Mind, sadness: The Cicuta patient, out of the convulsion, is full of sadness, anxiety, and darkness, borrows trouble from the future, is affected by sad stories, is pessimistic.
